macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Market Rallies on Trade Optimism and AI Demand
items.summary: The Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq surged following renewed optimism from a U.S.-China trade summit and strong AI sector performance. The Dow Jones climbed over 260 points with technology stocks leading gains, particularly Nvidia and Cisco.

items.title: Cisco Surges 14% on Earnings Beat and AI Restructuring
items.summary: Cisco Systems jumped to $116.40 after posting third-quarter results that beat Wall Street estimates on revenue and guidance, while announcing plans to cut nearly 4,000 jobs as part of a strategic shift toward AI-focused operations.

items.title: US Government Clears Chinese Companies to Purchase Nvidia AI Chips
items.summary: The United States government has approved approximately 10 Chinese companies to purchase Nvidia's H200 artificial intelligence chips, though actual deliveries have not yet commenced.

items.title: Initial Jobless Claims Rise Above Forecast
items.summary: Initial jobless claims came in at 211,000 for the week ending May 9, exceeding both the prior reading of 199,000 and the 205,000 forecast, while continuing claims rose by 24,000 to 1.78 million.

items.title: Fed May Abandon Rate-Cut Bias Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Seema Shah, Chief Global Strategist at Principal Asset Management, warns that investors are underpricing the prospect that the Federal Reserve may need to abandon its bias toward cutting interest rates.

items.title: April CPI Rises 3.8% Year-Over-Year
items.summary: The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers rose 0.6% seasonally adjusted in April 2026 and 3.8% over the past 12 months, indicating persistent inflation pressures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Jeff Clarke takes over Dell's PC business to revive growth
items.summary: Dell has put Jeff Clarke in charge of its PC business as the company works to reverse years of drift and better compete in a tougher hardware market. The move centers on restoring the XPS brand and sharpening Dell’s focus on fundamentals in the PC era.

items.title: Amazon expands Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is bringing Alexa+ to the web, extending its assistant beyond dedicated devices and into browser-based use. The rollout is part of a broader push to compete more directly in the digital assistant and agentic workflow market.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to bring Android PCs to the desktop, aiming to unify mobile and personal computing around Android. The effort is being positioned as a challenge to Microsoft and Apple in the PC market.

items.title: Quantum Motion unveils first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer made using standard silicon chip manufacturing techniques. The milestone could help make future quantum systems more scalable and manufacturable.
